profile.role: data protection officer / privacy compliance practitioner
profile.primary_lens: actionable remediation, audit-note quality, and developer communication

taxonomy_overall.summary: The taxonomy would make audit notes more actionable, especially when communicating with developers. It should include severity and suggested remediation for each code.

practitioner_code_comments.M1: Useful for explaining missing sharing disclosures. I need to know whether the third party is an independent recipient, ad network, analytics provider, or processor.
practitioner_code_comments.M2: Very useful for developer feedback. Developers often do not realise that advertising ID or Android ID belongs under Device or other IDs.
practitioner_code_comments.M3: Useful as a warning that the policy is too vague, but I would not cite it alone as a compliance issue.
practitioner_code_comments.M4: Most actionable. If the label says no data and the policy says otherwise, this is easy to explain and should be prioritised.
practitioner_code_comments.M5: Useful but less common. Needs examples because precise vs approximate location can be subtle.
practitioner_code_comments.M6: Could be useful in audit training, but I would need a very clear rule to avoid confusing security statements with data disclosures.
practitioner_code_comments.M7: Important when policies push users to third-party policies instead of disclosing app data flows. Needs care because many policies include this language legitimately.

severity_matrix_requested.comment: A severity matrix would make PRISM much easier to use in a real compliance workflow.

workflow_feedback.desired_output: For each flagged code, show evidence sentence, reason, severity, and suggested developer fix.
workflow_feedback.developer_facing_language_example: The policy mentions Android Advertising ID, but the Data Safety label does not declare Device or other IDs. Please update the label or clarify the policy scope.

exit_feedback.condition_for_adoption: The workbench must preserve human review and avoid presenting code triggers as final findings.
exit_feedback.free_text: PRISM is most valuable as a structured explanation layer. I would use it to triage cases and write clearer remediation requests. I would not use it as a standalone compliance decision system.
